revision.text:
[[ja:針間鴨国造]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crest_caption = | original_name = [[Yamabe no Muraji]] or {{ill|Radical 46|en|Radical 46|qid=Q99671|lt=Yama no Kimi clan|1=Radical 46}} | founder = [[Ichiriwake no Mikoto]] | type = {{ill|Imperial clan (Japan)|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial clan (Japan)|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581000}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} | base = Harima Kamo Province
(later {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}} {{ill|Kamo District, Harima|ja|賀茂郡 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo District, Harima|1=Kamo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634953}}, {{ill|Taka District, Hyōgo|en|Taka District, Hyōgo|qid=Q1042243|lt=Taka District, Hyōgo|1=Taka District, Hyōgo}}) | notable_members = See {{#section|#Notable Figures|Notable Figures}} | branches = {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108487214}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361048}} }} '''Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (針間鴨国造) governed {{ill|Harima Kamo Province|ja|針間鴨国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the northern region of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} appointed [[Ichiriwake no Mikoto]], a son of {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} and a relative of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, as the Kuni no Miyatsuko of Harima Kamo. === Clan === The clan is believed to be the {{ill|Radical 46|en|Radical 46|qid=Q99671|lt=Yamabe no Muraji|1=Radical 46}} (Yama clan), with the kabane (title) of ''Muraji.'' The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' lists the Yamabe clan under miscellaneous surnames of Kawachi Province and attributes their lineage to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. The Harima Kamo clan is related to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} ({{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108487214}}). The ''{{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}'' describes the ancestor {{ill|Okizagamio|ja|息長命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okizagamio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who mediated between {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}. Okizagamio, also known as ''Ishiji'' or ''Ōnaka Ishiji,'' is said to have married Hisurabi Hime of the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and founded the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine|ja|住吉酒見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉神社 (加西市)}}. His descendants, known as Yamasakato no Muraji, served as priests of the shrine. The {{ill|Saho Shrine|ja|佐保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho Shrine|qq=}} is believed to have been established by Abe Saburōtayū, a descendant of Harima Kamo no Miyatsuko. The clan’s {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomonomiyatsuko|qid=Q11381545}} branch is the {{ill|Yamabe no Muraji|ja|山部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A prominent figure from this branch is {{ill|Yamabe no Muraji Kotataki|ja|山部連小楯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe no Muraji Kotataki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Fukushima Yoshikazu|title=On the Saeki no Muraji Clan of Harima Province|journal=Jinbun Ronkyu|publisher=Kwansei Gakuin University|volume=45|issue=1|pages=1–15|date=1995|url=https://hdl.handle.net/10236/4985|issn=0286-6773}} == Base == === Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the territory known as ''Harima Kamo Province,'' which corresponded to {{ill|Kamo District, Harima|ja|賀茂郡 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo District, Harima|1=Kamo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634953}} and {{ill|Taka District, Hyōgo|en|Taka District, Hyōgo|qid=Q1042243|lt=Taka District, Hyōgo|1=Taka District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}. This area roughly aligns with the modern {{ill|North Harima|ja|北播磨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Harima|1=North Harima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20037372}} region of {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, encompassing {{ill|Ono, Hyōgo|en|Ono, Hyōgo|qid=Q911717|lt=Ono, Hyōgo|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|qid=Q851155|lt=Kasai, Hyōgo|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Katō, Hyōgo|en|Katō, Hyōgo|qid=Q877164|lt=Kato, Hyōgo|1=Katō, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Nishiwaki, Hyōgo|en|Nishiwaki, Hyōgo|qid=Q869020|lt=Nishiwaki, Hyōgo|1=Nishiwaki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Taka District, Hyōgo|en|Taka District, Hyōgo|qid=Q1042243|lt=Taka, Hyōgo|1=Taka District, Hyōgo}}, and parts of {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|qid=Q1042045|lt=Kamigō, Hyōgo|1=Kanzaki District, Hyōgo}}. The area includes the {{ill|Tamaoka Kofun Cluster|en|Tamaoka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11572357|lt=Tamaoka Kofun Cluster|1=Tamaoka Kofun Cluster}} in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|qid=Q851155|lt=Kasai, Hyōgo|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}. == Clan Shrine == The clan's shrine may have been either the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|qid=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} or the {{ill|Saho Shrine|ja|佐保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Kamo District, Harima|ja|賀茂郡 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo District, Harima|1=Kamo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634953}}. === Burial Site === * '''Tamaoka Kofun Cluster''' * Located in the towns of Shinke, Tamaoka, and Tamano in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|qid=Q851155|lt=Kasai, Hyōgo|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, the cluster includes middle-period Kofun structures such as {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝式古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 帆立貝形古墳}}, and [[Round Kofun|circular mounds]]. The ''Harima Fudoki'' contains myths related to the Tamaoka area and the clan's progeny. == Notable Figures == * '''Kuroda Wake''' (黒田別) ** Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. Mentioned in the ''Harima Fudoki.'' * '''Koma''' (許麻) ** Father of Nehime no Mikoto. Mentioned in the ''Harima Fudoki.'' * '''Nehime no Mikoto''' (根日女命) ** Daughter of Koma. According to the ''Harima Fudoki,'' Nehime declined the marriage proposals of two brothers, leading to her passing without marrying. * '''Unnamed Yamabe no Muraji''' ** A figure from Kamo District. === ''Kitadera Knowledge Sutra'' === The ''Kitadera Knowledge Sutra,'' transcribed by Buddhist laypeople in Harima's Kamo District in the 6th year of Tenpyō (734), mentions eight individuals from the Yamabe clan, likely descendants of Harima Kamo no Miyatsuko.Matsumoto Nobumichi, ''A Study of Upāsakas and Upāsikās in the Nara Period,'' Komazawa University. [http://repo.komazawa-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/all/20439/KJ00005098071.pdf] These individuals are: * Yamabe no Muraji Enetsu * Yamabe no Muraji Omi Furu * Yamabe no Muraji Mamoru * Yamabe no Muraji Ochime * Yamabe no Muraji Asamoru * Yamabe no Muraji Komamoru * Yamabe no Muraji Enshi * Yamabe no Muraji Yamamochi == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} {{translated page|ja|針間鴨国造|version=101232679|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:針間鴨国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487215}} [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
